---
title: "Slide Library"
subtitle: "Quarto HTML decks for Applied Math Lab"
---

These decks replace the legacy PowerPoint files in `slides/` with Quarto sources that render to HTML. Each presentation is short by design. The full mathematical detail remains in the module pages.

See the source files `slides/template-instructions.md` and `slides/_session-template.qmd` when you create a new deck.

## Available Decks

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
### Session 1
[ODEs in 1D](slides/01_ode_1d.qmd)

Solver workflow, SIR, saturation, and bistability.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 2
[ODEs in 2D](slides/02_ode_2d.qmd)

Phase planes, nullclines, limit cycles, and excitable dynamics.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 3
[Coupled ODEs](slides/03_ode_coupled.qmd)

Kuramoto synchronization, order parameter, and bifurcation onset.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 4
[Collective Motion](slides/04_collective_motion.qmd)

Vicsek dynamics, order-disorder transitions, and predator extensions.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 5
[Networks](slides/05_networks.qmd)

Graph structure, centrality, spreading, and real datasets.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 6
[PDEs in 1D](slides/06_pde_1d.qmd)

Boundary conditions, vectorized Laplacians, explicit Euler, Turing space, and unstable modes.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 7
[PDEs in 2D](slides/07_pde_2d.qmd)

Rectangular grids, mode pairs, Gierer-Meinhardt in 2D, and the Gray-Scott lab.
:::

::: {.card}
### Session 10
[GitHub Teamwork](slides/10_github_teamwork.qmd)

Repository setup, branching, pull requests, and issues.
:::

::: {.card}
### Reusable Template
See the source files `slides/template-instructions.md` and `slides/_session-template.qmd` when you create a new deck.
:::
:::

## How to Use Them

- Open a deck source in `slides/` and render it with Quarto.
- Keep the deck concise and point students to the related module pages for the full story.
- When you update a module, revise the matching deck in the same commit if the lecture flow changes.
